zh.lifeSummary: 當Dietrich IV van Beveren van Dixmude於大約1215年出生時，他的父親Dietrich III van Beveren32歲，母親Elisabeth de Wallers26歲。他和Marguerite de Brienne-Ramerupt至少有2個兒子和3個女兒。他去世於1274年12月13日在法國諾爾-帕-德-卡拉斯諾爾德瓦倫希恩斯享年60歲，埋葬在蒙古ЗавханТэс。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Dietrich IV van Beveren van Dixmude于大约1215年出生时，他的父亲Dietrich III van Beveren32岁，母亲Elisabeth de Wallers26岁。他和Marguerite de Brienne-Ramerupt至少有2个儿子和3个女儿。他去世于1274年12月13日在法国諾爾-帕-德-卡拉斯諾爾德瓦倫希恩斯享年60岁，埋葬在蒙古ЗавханТэс。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. nameMeanings.name: Dietrich
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

nameMeanings.name: IV
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Vivian   and   Vivien .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: When Dietrich IV van Beveren van Dixmude was born about 1215, his father, Dietrich III van Beveren, was 32 and his mother, Elisabeth de Wallers, was 26. He had at least 2 sons and 3 daughters with Marguerite de Brienne-Ramerupt. He died on 13 December 1274, in Valenciennes, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, France, at the age of 60, and was buried in Tes, Zavkhan, Mongolia.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Dietrich IV van Beveren van Dixmude est né environ 1215, son père, Dietrich III van Beveren, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Elisabeth de Wallers, avait 26 ans. Il a eu au moins 2 fils et 3 filles avec Marguerite de Brienne-Ramerupt. Il est décédé le 13 décembre 1274, à Valenciennes, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, France, à l’âge de 60 ans, et a été enterré à Tes, Dzavhan, Mongolie.

de.lifeSummary: Als Dietrich IV van Beveren van Dixmude um 1215 geboren wurde, war sein Vater Dietrich III van Beveren 32 und seine Mutter Elisabeth de Wallers 26. Er hatte mit Marguerite de Brienne-Ramerupt mindestens 2 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb am 13. Dezember 1274 im Alter von 60 Jahren in Valenciennes, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Frankreich und wurde in Tes, Dzavhan, Mongolei begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Dietrich IV van Beveren van Dixmude è nato il circa 1215, il suo padre, Dietrich III van Beveren, aveva 32 anni e il suo madre, Elisabeth de Wallers, aveva 26 anni. Lui avuto almeno 2 figli e 3 figlie con Marguerite de Brienne-Ramerupt. Morì il 13 dicembre 1274, nel Valenciennes, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Francia, all'età di 60 anni, e fu sepolto a Tes, Dzavhan, Mongolia.

ja.Diksmuide, Diksmuide, West Flanders, Bel: ベルギーウエスト-vラアンデレンディクスムイド
ja.Ieper, Ieper, West-Vlaanderen, België: ベルギーウエスト-vラアンデレンイエペル
ja.Valenciennes, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais,: フランスノード-プアス-デ-カレイスノードベレンシエンス
ja.lifeSummary: Dietrich IV van Beveren van Dixmudeが約1215年に生まれた時，父Dietrich III van Beverenは32歳，母Elisabeth de Wallersは26歳であった。彼はMarguerite de Brienne-Rameruptとの間に少なくとも2人の息子と3人の娘をもうけた。彼は1274年12月13日にフランスノード-プアス-デ-カレイスノードベレンシエンスで60歳で死去し，モンゴルЗавханТэсにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Dietrich IV van Beveren van Dixmude(이)가 대략 1215년에 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Dietrich III van Beveren(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Elisabeth de Wallers(은)는 26세였다. 그는 Marguerite de Brienne-Ramerupt(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1274년 12월 13일 프랑스 노르드-파스-데-캘레이 노르드 발시네스에서 60세의 나이로 사망했으며 몽골 Завхан Тэс에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Dietrich IV van Beveren van Dixmude nasceu aproximadamente 1215, seu pai, Dietrich III van Beveren, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Elisabeth de Wallers, tinha 26 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 2 filhos e 3 filhas com Marguerite de Brienne-Ramerupt. Ele faleceu em 13 de dezembro de 1274, em Valenciennes, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, França, com 60 anos, e foi sepultado em Tes, Dzavhan, Mongólia.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Dietrich IV van Beveren van Dixmude родился около 1215 г., его отцу по имени Dietrich III van Beveren было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Elisabeth de Wallers, 26. У него было по крайней мере 2 сына и 3 дочери от Marguerite de Brienne-Ramerupt. Он умер 13 декабря 1274 г. в местности веиленсиеннесь, норд, норд-пас-де-калеи, Франция в возрасте 60 лет и был похоронен в местности Тэс, Завхан, Монголия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Dietrich IV van Beveren van Dixmude nació aproximadamente 1215, su papá, Dietrich III van Beveren, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Elisabeth de Wallers, tenía 26 años. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos y 3 hijas con Marguerite de Brienne-Ramerupt. Murió el 13 de diciembre de 1274, en Valenciennes, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Francia, a la edad de 60 años, y fue sepultado en Tes, Dzavhan, Mongolia.
